steps.title: Compare two sunspot counts
steps.content: The passage supports the idea that sunspot numbers vary greatly by placing a high count, about 180 in November 2001, next to a low count, about 2 in December 2008.

steps.title: Use the comparison transition
steps.content: The matching transition introduces the second count as a comparison with the first. Choice A signals restatement, Choice B signals similarity, and Choice C signals cause and effect, none of which fits two contrasting examples. The answer is D.
